comments.text: hahaha mate, joins the club! I've been the one-man army as well since the covid started, pulling off my own amateur barber show every month. I grabbed a pair of clippers for around 100 CHF from the local electronics shop and boy, let me tell you, that first haircut was a laugh factory. Had a half-decent mullet going for a short while

thankfully with time I've actually gotten pretty decent. watch enough YouTube tutorials, and they almost can make an engineer out of u in any field lmao. plus, saves me from the eye watering Zurich barbershop prices. gotta love swiss living amirite? having high income definitely doesn't mean I'm looking to throw it away plus more $$$ for my reddit gold subscription and other online goodies
anyone else tried cutting their own hair and have some funny/ disastrous stories to share? or any tips for a better self-haircut are welcome too. gotta love the WaldGÃ¤nger lifestyle at times.

income.inference: The comments provide several clues about the author's financial situation. The author mentions living in Zurich, Switzerland, which is known for its high cost of living. They also refer to "eye watering Zurich barbershop prices" and "high income," suggesting they earn a substantial amount. The use of "CHF" (Swiss Francs) indicates they are likely residing in Switzerland. The mention of having more money for "reddit gold subscription and other online goodies" implies discretionary spending, which aligns with a higher income bracket. The author also jokes about making an engineer out of oneself through YouTube tutorials, which might suggest a technical or professional background, potentially associated with higher earnings. Overall, the language and context suggest the author is financially comfortable and likely earns a high income.

comments.text: oh man, i can relate an unfortunate hair experience. i've had mine cut into what can only be described as 'helmet hair' once. it took a good month to grow back into a more, shall we say, 'human' look. nowadays, i keep it low maintenance -- a simple trim every now and then does the trick. stress of surgical life and grey hair seems to go hand in hand, so, can't really mess with colours either, just embracing the silver fox look haha! lesson learned anyway: sometimes simpler is better.

gender.inference: The comment mentions an "unfortunate hair experience" with a reference to "helmet hair," which is a gender-neutral issue. However, the mention of embracing the "silver fox look" traditionally leans towards male terminology, as "silver fox" is often used to describe an older man who is seen as attractive due to his grey hair. There is no explicit mention of traditionally feminine hair concerns, such as styling, coloring (other than not being able to mess with colors due to grey hair), or length beyond "a simple trim." The reference to "surgical life" is not gender-specific, as both men and women work in surgical professions. The language used is casual and does not lean heavily towards one gender.
gender.certainty:
3

comments.text: oh man, i can relate an unfortunate hair experience. i've had mine cut into what can only be described as 'helmet hair' once. it took a good month to grow back into a more, shall we say, 'normal' look. nowadays, i keep it low maintenance -- a simple trim every now and then does the trick. stress of professional life and grey hair seems to go hand in hand, so, can't really mess with colours either, just embracing the natural look haha! lesson learned anyway: sometimes simpler is better.
